# Tend

> Tend your agents. Stay in flow.

Tend is a pull-based CLI for developers running multiple AI agents. Some agents need you every few minutes. Others run for hours untouched. The craft is knowing when each one needs you — and engineering more of them not to. One glance keeps you present when it matters, free when it doesn't.

## Key Facts

- Single binary, zero dependencies, no daemon, no database
- Five agent states: working, done, stuck, waiting, idle
- Agents emit events to `.tend/events` (plain text, append-only)
- Shell prompt glyph (○ / ?N ◐N ◉N) provides ambient certainty
- Works locally and remotely via an optional relay
- MIT licensed

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/metalaureate/tend-cli
- Install: curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metalaureate/tend-cli/main/install.sh | sh
- Docs: https://github.com/metalaureate/tend-cli/blob/main/README.md
- Full LLM context: https://tend.dev/llms-full.txt

## Quick Start

```
cd ~/projects/my-app
td init
td            # show the board from anywhere
```

## Commands

- td                     — Show the board
- td watch                   — Live dashboard (auto-refreshes every minute)
- td <project>           — Project detail + agent sessions
- td <N>                 — Project detail by board number
- td #<N>                — Switch to project N
- td init [project]      — Initialize .tend/ in a project
- td add [project] "msg" — Queue a TODO for the agent
- td ack [project]       — Clear done/stuck/waiting → idle
- td status              — Shell prompt indicator (○ or ?N ◐N ◉N)
- td remove [project]    — Remove tend from a project
- td relay <subcmd>      — Manage relay (setup|status|pull|token)
- td emit <state> "msg"  — Emit an event (used by agents)
- td help                — Show help
